body:
## Goal
Provide a dependency-free JSON REST API where users sign up, sign in for an opaque bearer session, and create and list their own notes, with all users and notes persisted on disk so they survive a server restart.

## Spec
Built on Node's standard library only — `node:http` for the server/router and `node:sqlite` (`DatabaseSync`, available in Node 22.5+/24) for an embedded on-disk SQLite file — with no external web framework or runtime dependency.

Current state (verified 2026-07-23): the repo is an empty ESM package (`"type": "module"`, Node v24) whose only scripts are `typecheck` (`node --check` sweep), `lint`, and `test` (`node --test`); there is no `src/` and no `start` script yet.

Layout and behavior:
- `src/db.js` — opens/creates the SQLite file (path from `DB_PATH`, default `data/app.db`; `data/` created if missing), runs idempotent `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` DDL for `users` (id, username UNIQUE, password_hash, salt), `sessions` (token, user_id), and `notes` (id, user_id, title, body, created_at), and exposes small query helpers. Opening an existing file reuses stored rows, which is what delivers restart survival.
- Passwords are never stored in plaintext: hash with a per-user random salt using `node:crypto` `scrypt`; `/login` verifies with a constant-time compare.
- Sessions: `/login` mints an opaque random token (`crypto.randomBytes` hex) persisted in `sessions`; protected routes read `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, resolve it to a user, and 401 when absent/invalid.
- `src/app.js` — a `(req, res)` handler that parses the JSON body (rejecting non-JSON / oversized bodies), routes on method+path, and always responds `application/json`. Routes: `POST /signup` → `{ id, username }` (409 on duplicate username, 400 on missing/blank fields); `POST /login` → `{ session }` (401 on bad credentials); `GET /me` → `{ id, username }` (auth required); `POST /notes` → the created note object (auth required, 400 on missing fields); `GET /notes` → JSON array of the caller's own notes only (auth required). Unknown route → 404; unsupported method → 405.
- `src/server.js` — imports the handler and calls `listen(process.env.PORT || 3000)`; this is the `start` entry.
- `package.json` — add `"start": "node src/server.js"`.

Multi-user isolation is enforced by scoping every notes query to the authenticated `user_id`, so one user can never read or write another's notes.

## Changes
- `src/db.js` — creates
- `src/app.js` — creates
- `src/server.js` — creates
- `package.json` — refactors-existing
- `test/api.test.js` — creates
- `.gitignore` — refactors-existing

## Acceptance
- [ ] AC-1: `npm start` boots an HTTP server that listens on the port from `PORT` (defaulting to 3000 when `PORT` is unset).
- [ ] AC-2: `POST /signup` with JSON `{"username","password"}` responds with JSON containing the new user's `id` and `username`; a duplicate username is rejected with a 4xx status rather than creating a second account.
- [ ] AC-3: `POST /login` with correct credentials responds with JSON `{"session": <opaque token>}`; wrong credentials respond 401 without a session.
- [ ] AC-4: `GET /me` with `Authorization: Bearer <session>` returns JSON with the signed-in user's `id` and `username`; the same request with a missing or invalid token responds 401.
- [ ] AC-5: `POST /notes` with a valid session and JSON `{"title","body"}` responds with the created note (including its title and body); no session responds 401.
- [ ] AC-6: `GET /notes` with a valid session returns a JSON array of only the calling user's notes — a second user's notes are never included.
- [ ] AC-7: Malformed input (non-JSON body, or signup/note missing required fields) responds with a 400 rather than a 500 or a crash.
- [ ] AC-8: Users and notes persist across a process restart: data written before stopping the server is readable through the API after the store is re-opened (the on-disk store is a file, not in-memory).
- [ ] AC-9: Passwords are not stored in plaintext in the on-disk store.

## Verify
- npm test (unit)
- npm run typecheck (validate)

## References
- `README.md` — exists

## Non-Goals
- No external web framework, ORM, or third-party runtime dependency (standard library only).
- No token expiry, refresh, logout, note update/delete, or pagination — only the endpoints named in Acceptance.
- No HTTPS/TLS termination or rate limiting (deployment concerns out of scope).

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"Deliver the complete signup/login/session + personal-notes JSON API on a persistent on-disk store in one coherent, self-verifying pass (c: one cohesive capability, splitting it would break the auth+notes+persistence contract).","estimated_test_files":1} -->

<!-- plan-story: 3ac0eedf71b2dcad -->
title: JSON REST API for user accounts and personal notes (Node ESM, on-disk store)
